headline: Auditor General flagged same 6 ministries for 5 consecutive years
context: The Auditor General's 2025 report repeated findings of financial mismanagement in the same six ministries flagged annually since 2021, with no corrective action taken.

cards.big: Same 6 ministries failed audits 5 years in a row — nothing changed
cards.sub: Repeat offenders include Works, Rural Development, Youth, Agriculture, Women, and Tourism

cards.big: AG's reports are tabled in Parliament but have no enforcement mechanism
cards.sub: PAC can recommend but cannot compel ministries to implement corrections

cards.big: Combined irregular spending across 6 ministries totalled RM1.7 billion in 2025
cards.sub: Includes overpayments, phantom projects, and unapproved budget variations

cards.big: Only 23% of AG recommendations from 2021 have been implemented
cards.sub: 77% of corrective measures were ignored or partially addressed after four years

cards.big: An audit without consequences is just expensive record-keeping of government failure
Should ministers face automatic review if their ministry fails audits 3 years running?
